metadata.title: Claude, On Schedule — bot vs bot, season one · 2/7
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity UI beats; exhibits keep their own look (MUSINIQUE.md graded-exhibit law).
metadata.greeting_note: hello lexicon: Swahili (one-word budget — Musinique persona). Wagwan stays Bear's.
metadata.charter: MUSINIQUE.md — fights for the indie musician; receipts law; roll-your-own law; no fake numbers
metadata.note: ILLUSTRATE LAW: UI at B00 (with RESULT lines) / verdict / handoff / outro only; each inner beat carries an illustration spec in props.illustration (kind: concept | pattern | code | exhibit) — build per medhavy_com/youtube/mcp/remotion-src worked example + skills/animated-deck patterns. ROLL-YOUR-OWN LAW: the handoff paste is a deck piece the viewer keeps. RECEIPTS LAW: claims trace to RECEIPTS.md beside the playlist; illustrative examples labeled as such.

beats.narration_text: I want to try the Monday-morning digest before I trust anything bigger, and I keep circling the warning that scheduled tasks only run when the desktop app is awake. Help me build my first low-stakes recipe: (1) draft the exact digest prompt that pulls last week's releases, what moved, and three follow-ups, all in my voice file; (2) list what I must verify by hand before I read it as fact; (3) tell me how to tell a genuine missed-morning from an actual failure.

props.command: I want to try the Monday-morning digest before I trust anything bigger, and I keep circling the warning that scheduled tasks only run when the desktop app is awake. Help me build my first low-stakes recipe: (1) draft the exact digest prompt that pulls last week's releases, what moved, and three follow-ups, all in my voice file; (2) list what I must verify by hand before I read it as fact; (3) tell me how to tell a genuine missed-morning from an actual failure.
output: repurpose: one diary entry → every platform
Monday 9am: last week, summarized, with follow-ups
overnight: supervisor research, drafted — you send

beats.narration_text: The workhorse: the repurposing engine. Drop one studio-diary entry in a project folder. Out: a blog post, platform-fit captions, a short-video script — all pulled through your saved voice file, so it sounds like you and not like a brochure. One take, every platform. That's an hour a week back.

Start smaller than you want to. Practitioners keep landing on the same first recipe: a Monday-morning digest — what went out last week, what moved, three follow-ups worth making. Low stakes, runs weekly, and it teaches you whether you trust the machine before you hand it anything that matters.

The high-leverage one: overnight research. Which supervisors placed songs like yours in the last eighteen months. What the comparable artists shipped this week. Drafted pitches referencing your actual catalog. It runs while you sleep. And notice the stamp on everything: draft. You send. Nothing goes out under your name without your hand on it.

Now the fine print, because I fight for you, not for the software. Scheduled tasks run when the desktop app is awake — a missed morning is expected behavior, not a bug. Agentic runs burn more usage than chat, so batch your jobs. And when it reads a dashboard through a browser, verify the numbers. Always.

And the red line, which is the whole philosophy: Co-work drafts, you decide. Any number you haven't verified. Any message to a named human being. Any decision that spends money. Those never ride the schedule. The practitioners who got burned all report the same wound — a report that looked right and missed the point. The schedule buys you time. It does not buy you judgment.

Let's recap with Claude. One page. Three recipes that give you your evenings back, and one line that keeps your name safe: it drafts, you decide. That trade — machine hours for human judgment — is the only automation deal worth taking.

artifactLines: Repurposing engine: one source → blog + captions + scripts, through your saved voice file.
The Monday digest: last week summarized, three follow-ups — the trust-building first recipe.
Overnight supervisor research: placements found, pitches DRAFTED — a human sends.
The fine print: desktop must be awake · agentic runs cost more · verify anything read off a screen · drafts, never decisions.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Co-work with your content folder connected. The first run interviews you and writes your voice file — episode four sharpens it. From next Monday, the digest is yours.

props.command: Create my Monday 9am recipe: summarize everything I posted last week from this folder, pull what engagement you can verify, and suggest three follow-ups in my voice. Flag — never send — anything with a numeric claim or a named person. First run: interview me for my brand-voice file.
